creator: bahlgren@beah.se
text:
Hiroki Sato <hrs@FreeBSD.org> writes:

> Bengt Ahlgren <bengta@sics.se> wrote
>   in <200812101814.mBAIERYg071437@www.freebsd.org>:
>
> be> The following error occurred while printing...
> be> '/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: /usr/local/Adobe/Reader8/ENU/Adobe/Reader8/Reader/intellinux/lib/libstdc++.so.6: unsupported file layout'
> be>
> be> and on amd64:
> be>
> be> The following error occurred while printing...
> be> '/libexec/ld-elf.so.1: Shared object "libm.so.6" not found, required by "libstdc++.so.6"'
> be>
> be> The reason is that the acroread launch script sets LD_LIBRARY_PATH to:
>
>  What is the helper program which occurs the above error exactly?

KDE's kprinter program (which I entered as the print program in the
print dialogue).

(But I think I got the error messages swapped amd64<->i386)

Bengt
